headings: Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter: solid, liquid and gas and their characteristics; change of state- melting (absorption of heat), freezing, evaporation (cooling by evaporation), condensation, sublimation.

Elements, compounds and mixtures. Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures, colloids and suspensions. Physical and chemical changes (excluding separating the components of a mixture); Pure and Impure substances.

Atoms and molecules, Law of Chemical Combination, Chemical formula of common compounds, Atomic and molecular masses. Structure of atom: Sub-atomic particles: Electrons, protons and neutrons, Models of atom; Valency, Atomic Number and Mass Number, Isotopes and Isobars.
subtopics: States of matter, change of state
Sub-atomic particles, Models of atom, Isotopes and Isobars

headings: Cell as a basic unit of life; prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, multicellular organisms; cell membrane and cell wall, cell organelles and cell inclusions; chloroplast, mitochondria, vacuoles, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus; nucleus, chromosomes - basic structure, number.
Structure and functions of animal and plant tissues (only four types of tissues in animals; Meristematic and Permanent tissues in plants).

headings: Distance and displacement, velocity; uniform and non-uniform motion along a straight line; acceleration, distance-time and velocity-time graphs for uniform motion and uniformly accelerated motion, elementary idea of uniform circular motion.
Force and Motion, Newton's Laws of Motion, Action and Reaction forces, Inertia of a body, Inertia and mass, Momentum, Force and Acceleration.
Gravitation; Universal Law of Gravitation, Force of Gravitation of the earth (gravity), Acceleration due to Gravity; Mass and Weight; Free fall.
Thrust and Pressure. Archimedes' Principle; Buoyancy.
Work done by a Force, Energy, power; Kinetic and Potential energy; Lawof conservation of energy (excluding commercial unit of Energy).
Nature of sound and its propagation in various media, speed of sound, range of hearing in humans; ultrasound; reflection of sound; echo.
Plant and animal breeding and selection for quality improvement and management; Use of fertilizersand manures; Protection from pests and diseases; Organic farming.

headings: Chemical reactions, Chemical equation, Balanced chemical equation, types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction.

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH– ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale... importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris.
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention.

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature... Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid...

explicit_chapters: Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
headings: 'Living Being'. Basic concept of nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals.
Tropic movements in plants; Introduction of plant hormones; Control and co-ordination in animals: Nervous system; Voluntary, involuntary and reflex action; Chemical co-ordination: animal hormones.
Reproduction in animals and plants (asexual and sexual) reproductive health - need and methods of family planning. Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS. Child bearing and women's health.
Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits: Sex determination; brief introduction.

Laws of refraction, refractive index. Refraction of light by spherical lens; Image formed by spherical lenses; Lens formula (Derivation not required); Magnification. Power of a lens. Functioning of a lens in human eye, defects of vision and their corrections...
Refraction of light through a prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light...

practicals.title: Preparation of solutions (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 1. Preparation of: Unit-I a) a true solution of common salt, sugar and alum b) a suspension of soil, chalk powder and fine sand in water c) a colloidal solution of starch in water and egg albumin/milk in water
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: Preparation of mixture/compound (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 2. Preparation of Unit-I a) A mixture b) A compound using iron filings and sulphur powder
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: Physical/Chemical changes reactions (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 3. Perform the following reactions... Iron with copper sulphate solution... Burning of magnesium ribbon...
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: Stained temporary mounts (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 4. Preparation of stained temporary mounts of (a) onion peel, (b) human cheek cells
practicals.related_unit: Organization in the Living World (Unit II)

practicals.title: Identification of tissues (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 5. Identification of Parenchyma, Collenchyma and Sclerenyma tissues in plants... muscle fibers...
practicals.related_unit: Organization in the Living World (Unit II)

practicals.title: Melting/Boiling point determination (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 6. Determination of the melting point of ice and the boiling point of water.
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: Laws of reflection of sound (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 7. Verification of the laws of reflection of sound.

practicals.title: Density determination (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 8. Determination of the density of solid... by using a spring balance...

practicals.title: Archimedes' Principle verification (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 9. Establishing the relation between the loss in weight of a solid when fully immersed...

practicals.title: Speed of pulse propagation (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 10. Determination of the speed of a pulse propagated through a stretched string/slinky

practicals.title: Conservation of mass verification (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 11. Verification of the law of conservation of mass in a chemical reaction.
practicals.related_unit: Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)

practicals.title: pH determination (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 1. A. Finding the pH of the following samples by using pH paper/universal indicator: Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution...

practicals.title: Properties of acids/bases reactions (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 2. Performing and observing the following reactions... Action of water on quicklime...

practicals.title: Reactivity series experiment (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 3. Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on salt solutions...

practicals.title: Ohm's Law verification (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 4. Studying the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I)... plotting a graph between V and I.

practicals.title: Equivalent resistance (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 5. Determination of the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Stomata observation (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 6. Preparing a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: CO2 in respiration experiment (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 7. Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Acetic acid properties (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 8. Study of the following properties of acetic acid... Odour, solubility in water...

practicals.title: Soap cleaning capacity (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 9. Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.

practicals.title: Focal length determination (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 10. Determination of the focal length of: Concave mirror, Convex lens...

practicals.title: Glass slab refraction tracing (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 11. Tracing the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab...

practicals.title: Binary fission/Budding study (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 12. Studying (a) binary fission in Amoeba, and (b) budding in yeast...

practicals.title: Prism refraction tracing (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 13. Tracing the path of the rays of light through a glass prism.

practicals.title: Embryo identification (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 14. Identification of the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed...

activities.title: Periodic Assessment Methods
activities.source_text: Diverse methods of assessment as per the need of the class dynamics and curriculum transaction. These may include - short tests, oral test, quiz, concept maps, projects, posters, presentations and enquiry based scientific investigations etc.

projects.title: Portfolio
projects.source_text: For 5 marks - Portfolio that includes classwork and other sample of student's work.

marks_distribution: Annual Examination: 80 Marks (Class IX and X)
excluded_or_non_assessed_content: Information in NCERT text book boxes (not assessed in year-end examination)
Separating the components of a mixture (Physical and chemical changes section - Class IX Unit I)
Commercial unit of Energy (Work, Energy and Power section - Class IX Unit III)
Definition relating to logarithm for pH scale
teacher_notes: The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination.
Formative topics (Health and Diseases; Conservation of Momentum) are included to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.
Schools can integrate formative topics with existing chapters as they align well.
